title: Smoky Jamaican Eggs Stew
description: A creamy, smoky stew brimming with poached eggs and earthy spices, perfect for a lowfat, festive breakfast.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and diced bell pepper; cook until softened, about 2 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, ground cumin, and dried thyme; sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the diced tomatoes and vegetable broth, bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat and stir in the low‑fat coconut milk and lemon juice; let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
Gently slide the eggs into the simmering sauce, cover, and cook until the egg whites are set but yolks remain slightly runny, about 4–6 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, garnish with fresh cilantro (if desired), and serve hot.
